description: The repository provides a repeatable initialization and structural smoke check for future implementation sessions.
acceptanceSteps: Run ./init.sh in a clean checkout with Node.js and npm installed.
Confirm npm run check:structure exits successfully and reports the required harness artifacts.
Confirm the command does not provision a Daytona resource.
evidence: 2026-07-17: In a clean temporary copy without .git, node_modules, or .env, ./init.sh installed the locked dependencies and npm run check:structure reported 14 required files and 28 features. A fake daytona executable placed first on PATH was not invoked while ./init.sh completed successfully.

description: Control-plane and runner code share a validated, bounded normalized event contract.
acceptanceSteps: Compile the contracts package.
Run contract tests for valid text, tool, lifecycle, and error events.
Run contract tests proving malformed or oversized records are rejected.

description: Convex stores typed thread, sandbox session, message, run, and tool-execution records with required indexes and state transitions.
acceptanceSteps: Deploy or test the Convex schema successfully.
Verify indexes exist for messages by thread/sequence, tool executions by run/sequence, runs by thread/creation time, sandbox sessions by thread, and clientRequestId idempotency.
Verify invalid lifecycle transitions are rejected.

description: A responsive accessible Next.js chat shell displays a thread list, thread state, message list, and disabled/enabled composer from reactive Convex data.
acceptanceSteps: Start npm run dev and open the application.
Verify the thread list, New conversation control, message area, and composer render at desktop and tablet widths.
Verify keyboard focus reaches interactive controls and the composer is disabled for non-accepting states.

description: Creating a conversation records one thread and one provisioning intent per client request ID.
acceptanceSteps: Call thread creation twice with the same clientRequestId.
Verify one thread and one provisioning request are stored.
Call with a new clientRequestId and verify a new thread is stored.

description: Creating a conversation provisions one private Daytona VM-compatible sandbox from the configured prebuilt snapshot.
acceptanceSteps: Configure a Daytona target and pi-agent-v1 VM-compatible snapshot.
Create a thread and verify the provisioning action creates a private Daytona sandbox from that snapshot.
Record the sandbox ID, target, snapshot, and provisioning duration in Convex.

description: Two conversations receive different Daytona sandbox IDs.
acceptanceSteps: Create two threads with different client request IDs.
Wait until both are ready.
Verify the two sandbox session records contain non-empty, unequal Daytona sandbox IDs.

description: Conversation provisioning uses a prepared Daytona snapshot and never installs runner dependencies from scratch per thread.
acceptanceSteps: Build or validate the configured pi-agent-v1 snapshot contains the runner and pinned dependencies.
Provision a thread and inspect runner startup logs for absence of per-thread dependency installation.
Record the snapshot identifier with provisioning evidence.

description: The Pi Agent process itself runs inside the Daytona VM mapped to its conversation.
acceptanceSteps: Create a ready thread and submit a runner health or diagnostic turn.
Verify process/cwd evidence identifies the Pi runner inside the mapped Daytona sandbox.
Verify no host-side Pi process performs the agent turn.

description: Each runner owns a persistent Pi AgentSession and session files in its VM.
acceptanceSteps: Send two ordered turns to one ready thread.
Verify the second turn observes the first turn's session context.
Verify the Pi session file remains in the mapped VM filesystem.

description: The in-VM runner exposes authenticated normalized NDJSON events to the Convex control plane.
acceptanceSteps: Run a runner contract test that emits lifecycle, text, tool, and terminal NDJSON events.
Verify Convex validates and persists the events in order.
Verify an unauthenticated runner request is rejected.

description: Runner authentication uses dynamically generated per-thread tokens that are not client-readable.
acceptanceSteps: Provision two threads and inspect their secret references without logging token values.
Verify the references differ and no token is returned by a public Convex query.
Verify browser build output contains no Daytona, provider, search, or runner credential.

description: Assistant text is visible progressively before a turn completes and partial text remains visible after a failure.
acceptanceSteps: Submit a turn that emits more than one text delta.
Observe at least one persisted partial message update before the terminal run event.
Force a terminal error after text and verify the partial assistant message is retained.

description: Tool execution progress is progressively exposed as normalized ordered events.
acceptanceSteps: Run a turn that invokes a tool with progress or output chunks.
Verify a pending/running record appears before its terminal status.
Verify the terminal record includes bounded structured output or error details.

description: Pi exposes the exact bash tool inside its Daytona VM.
acceptanceSteps: Prompt Pi to invoke bash in the mapped VM.
Verify a structured bash execution record and bounded output are persisted.

description: Pi exposes the exact read, write, edit, and grep tools inside its Daytona VM.
acceptanceSteps: Prompt Pi to write a file, read it, edit it, and grep the resulting content in the mapped VM.
Verify four ordered structured tool records named write, read, edit, and grep.

description: Pi exposes the exact glob tool with real file-pattern matching semantics inside its Daytona VM.
acceptanceSteps: Create matching and non-matching files in the mapped VM.
Prompt Pi to invoke glob with a documented pattern.
Verify the structured glob output includes only matching paths.

description: Pi exposes the exact webfetch tool with bounded, structured HTTP/HTTPS retrieval safety controls.
acceptanceSteps: Fetch a permitted public HTTPS document and verify a bounded structured result with content-type handling.
Verify invalid schemes, a private or link-local address where practical, excessive redirects, and oversized responses are rejected or bounded.
Verify the execution record is named webfetch.

description: Pi exposes the exact websearch tool through a documented configured provider and returns no fabricated results.
acceptanceSteps: Configure a documented web-search provider such as Tavily.
Prompt Pi to invoke websearch and verify structured provider-backed results.
Remove the provider key and verify a structured configuration error rather than static or fabricated search data.

description: The UI makes each tool's name, input, output, status, execution order, timing, and error state inspectable.
acceptanceSteps: Complete a turn with at least two tool executions.
Open the observability panel and verify strictly increasing execution order plus start/end timing and status.
Expand both tool records and verify their input/output or error details are visible.

description: The UI shows provisioning, ready, running, stopped, and error states with VM ID, provisioning duration, time to first token, retry, and composer availability.
acceptanceSteps: Exercise each thread lifecycle state through tests or an integration environment.
Verify state-specific UI and composer behavior for each state.
Verify VM ID, provisioning duration, and time-to-first-token values render when available.

description: A thread accepts only one active run at a time while independent threads can run separately.
acceptanceSteps: Start a run on one ready thread.
Attempt a second distinct request on that thread and verify it is rejected or blocked without a second runner prompt.
Start a run on a different ready thread and verify it can proceed.

description: Duplicate client turn requests are idempotent and do not create duplicate runs or execution side effects.
acceptanceSteps: Send the same submit-message clientRequestId twice.
Verify one run, one user message, and one runner turn are recorded.
Verify a new clientRequestId creates a new eligible run after the first completes.

description: A thread's Pi agent cannot access another thread's Daytona VM files.
acceptanceSteps: Create ready threads A and B with different Daytona IDs.
Use thread A to write a unique sentinel in its VM.
Use thread B to attempt to read that sentinel and verify it cannot access it.

description: Stopping and resuming a mapped Daytona VM retains its filesystem and Pi session state.
acceptanceSteps: Write a VM sentinel and establish Pi session context for a ready thread.
Stop and resume the mapped Daytona sandbox.
Verify the same sandbox ID, sentinel, and session context are available after resume.

description: Daytona provisioning overhead from the prebuilt snapshot is measured and reported.
acceptanceSteps: Run the documented provisioning benchmark against the configured VM-compatible snapshot for the documented sample size.
Save raw start-to-ready timing samples with target and snapshot metadata.
Report summary statistics without claiming results from mocked runs.

description: A real Daytona-backed browser flow creates a thread, streams a Pi response from its VM, and exposes its execution history.
acceptanceSteps: Run the opt-in real Daytona integration test with configured credentials.
Run the Playwright end-to-end flow against the integration environment.
Verify cleanup outcome is recorded for test sandboxes.

description: The repository supplies consistent architecture documentation, environment guidance, GitHub source handoff, and a cap.so demo plan based on verified behavior.
acceptanceSteps: Review README, architecture, decisions, environment, and test evidence for consistency with the deployed system.
Verify no document claims an unverified real integration as complete.
Record a cap.so demo that shows thread creation, streaming, VM mapping, and tool observability.
